items.title: Will NATO countries shoot down Russian drones and jets? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Senior commanders from all 32 NATO member states have met in Riga for talks following a spate of suspected Russian drone incursions. European countries are on high alert after drones flew over civilian and military infrastructure in Denmark, Germany and Norway. Denmark says the drone activity above several airports across the country had the hallmark of ‘professional actors’. Russian drones without explosive payloads fell on Poland earlier this month. 

00:00 Senior commanders from all 32 NATO member states meet in Riga
01:03 Christopher Morris, Defense Analyst
06:01 Report: Drone training programs for children in Lithuania

items.title: Nigeria: Coastal erosion threatens entire communities | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Along the coast of the commercial hub of Lagos, Nigeria, communities are battling an ever-shrinking shoreline. Climate change and coastal development have both been blamed. DW's Olisa Chukwumah went to Apakin, where residents fear the extinction of their villages.

items.title: Chokepoints: How small waterways make big powers vulnerable | Mapped Out
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Recent Houthi attacks on ships in the Red Sea are a stark reminder: Ships are vulnerable in and near maritime chokepoints. This is a headache for big powers that rely on flows on trade and oil. The Panama Canal, the Strait of Malacca in the Indo-Pacific and the Danish Straits on the Baltic Sea are thorns in the side of the US, China and Russia. That’s why their leaders, Donald Trump, Xi Jinping, and Vladimir Putin are moving to protect their interests and escape this vulnerability.

Chapters:
00:00 Trouble in the Red Sea 
00:48 Chokepoints in global trade and conflicts 
02:57 China’s Malacca Dilemma 
05:05 Russia’s shadow fleet in the Danish Straits  
08:00 The US’ Panama Canal problem 
10:40 Superpower strategies  

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Rwanda hosts road cycling world championships amid human-rights concerns | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The Road Cycling World Championships are underway in Kigali, Rwanda. For the first time in 103 years, the event is being held on African soil. And, this year's event is not just for the pros. Amateurs are also competing alongside the sport’s biggest names. This has allowed for greater participation from African riders who are taking despite limited resources and logistical challenges.

00:00 Road Cycling World Championships in Rwanda
01:55 DW speaks with former professional road racing cyclist Nic Dlamini, who was the first Black South African to compete in the Tour de France

items.title: Argentina outraged after cartel livestreams triple femicide | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Argentinians are outraged as the grisly details of a triple femicide in the outskirts of Buenos Aires emerge. Two women and a teenage girl were tortured and murdered on livestream by a narcotics gang while dozens watched, according to police. The three victims had been lured by the promise of money in exchange for sex and then killed as a "warning" for allegedly stealing cocaine. A relative of two of the victims said it was society's fault for "pointing the finger" at his cousins for engaging in sex work in order to survive.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: NATO defense chiefs gather in Latvia to discuss Russia's airspace violations | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In Latvia, armed forces chiefs from all 32 NATO member states are meeting in Riga to discuss their collective deterrence and defense on the Alliance’s eastern flank, after recent incidents involving Russian violations of NATO airspace. This week, NATO fighter jets scrambled after Russian warplanes were spotted near Latvian airspace over the Baltic Sea, prompting fresh concerns among allies about Moscow’s repeated airspace violations. DW's Juri Rescheto reports from Riga.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Netanyahu to meet with Trump on heels of defiant Gaza speech at UN | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu struck a defiant tone as he addressed the United Nations in New York, vowing to "finish the job" against Hamas in Gaza. Dozens of diplomats and officials staged a walkout before Netanyahu began speaking. In a fiery speech, he repeated his vow that there will never be a Palestinian state. Netanyahu is to meet with US President Donald Trump at the White House on Monday.

00:00 Netanyahu delivers defiant speech at UN
02:36 Journalist Karim El-Gawhary reports from Cairo
08:34 DW speaks with Fawaz Gerges from the Middle-Eastern Politics and International Relations Department at London School of Economics

items.title: Is Trump trying to shift responsibility for Ukraine to Europe? | To the Point
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>It was a speech full of self-praise that - US President Donald Trump - delivered before the UN General Assembly. His assertion that Ukraine could reclaim occupied territories has raised eyebrows around the world - and hopes - in Kyiv. So, is Trump really distancing himself further from Moscow? Or is this just another chapter in his unpredictable policy book? Trump is making it clear - not just at the UN, but across the board that it’s only his worldview that matters. At home, he continues to reshape the United States to his own vision. After the murder of far-right activist Charlie Kirk, the President is cracking down harder on his critics. The press - both domestic and international—is facing massive hostility. Critics warn that the U.S. may have already crossed the threshold into authoritarianism. So, today, on To The Point, we ask: Trump the Unpredictable: What’s His Real Plan for Ukraine and for Democracy?

Watch the full episode here: https://youtu.be/UKx2LnkCeAM

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Live: Sergey Lavrov, others speak at United Nations | UN General Assembly Day 5
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>List of speakers, morning session:
1 Bahamas: His Excellency Philip Edward Davis, Prime Minister and Minister for Finance of the Commonwealth of the Bahamas
2 Niger: His Excellency Lamine Zeine Ali Mahaman, Prime Minister of the Transition Government and Minister of Economy and Finance of the Republic of the Niger
3 Grenada: His Excellency Dickon Mitchell, Prime Minister and Minister for Infrastructure and Physical Development, Public Utilities, Civil Aviation and Transportation and Minister for National Security, Home Affairs, Public Administration, Information and Disaster Management of Grenada
4 Burkina Faso : His Excellency Rimtalba Jean Emmanuel Ouedraogo, Prime Minister, Head of Government of Burkina Faso
5 Saint Kitts and Nevis : His Excellency Terrance Micheal Drew, Prime Minister and Minister for Finance, National Security and Immigration, Health, and Social Security of Saint Kitts and Nevis
6 Lao People's Democratic Republic : His Excellency Sonexay Siphandone, Prime Minister of the Lao People's Democratic Republic
7 Armenia : His Excellency Nikol Pashinyan, Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia
8 Cambodia : His Excellency Sokhonn Prak, Deputy Prime Minister, Minister for Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation of Cambodia
9 Russian Federation : His Excellency Sergey Lavrov, Minister for Foreign Affairs of the Russian Federation
10 Cuba : His Excellency Bruno Eduardo Rodríguez Parrilla, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Cuba
11 Brunei Darussalam : His Excellency Setia Awang Haji Erywan bin Pehin Datu Pekerma Jaya Haji Mohd Yusof, Minister for Foreign Affairs II of Brunei Darussalam
12 Germany : His Excellency Johann Wadephul, Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs of Germany
13 Iceland : Her Excellency Thorgerdur Katrín Gunnarsdóttir, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Iceland
14 India : His Excellency Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, Minister for External Affairs of India
15 Egypt : His Excellency Badr Ahmed Mohamed Abdelatty, Minister for Foreign Affairs, Emigration and Egyptian Expatriates of Egypt
16 Thailand : His Excellency Sihasak Phuangketkeow, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Thailand
17 Belarus : His Excellency Maxim Ryzhenkov, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Belarus
18 Mauritania: His Excellency Mohamed Salem Ould Merzoug, Minister for Foreign Affairs, African Cooperation and Mauritanians Abroad of Mauritania

Afternoon session:
1 United Arab Emirates : His Highness Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Foreign Affairs of the United Arab Emirates
2 San Marino : His Excellency Luca Beccari, Minister for Foreign Affairs, Political Affairs, International Economic Cooperation and Digital Transition of San Marino
3 Saudi Arabia : His Highness Faisal bin Farhan Al Saud, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Saudi Arabia
4 Singapore : His Excellency Vivian Balakrishnan, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Singapore

6 Malaysia : His Excellency Mohamad Hasan, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Malaysia
7 Maldives : His Excellency Abdulla Khaleel, Minister for Foreign Affairs of Maldives
